name: Kiwi Valley Loop
description: Easy 17.0-mile (27.4 km) mountain bike loop around Peyrehorade, offering varied terrain through local countryside.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Orthevielle traverse a diverse landscape characterized by wooded parcels, agricultural fields, and kiwi orchards. The region, situated in the Pays d'Orthe, offers varied terrain for cycling, from flat plains to gentle rises. The Gaves Réunis river defines the southern border, providing scenic routes. While Orthevielle itself offers more gentle rides, its location provides access to more challenging mountainous areas further afield. Best mountain bike trails around Orthevielle The most popular mountain bike trail is Vers le château de Gramont à Came, an easy 18.9 miles (30.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete, offering views towards Château de Gramont. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Kiwi Valley Loop, an easy 17.0 miles (27.4 km) path. This route winds through the distinctive kiwi orchards and agricultural plains of the region. Local mountain bikers also love the Cannon at Pont de Laxague – View of Château de Gramont loop from Sames, a 14.0 miles (22.5 km) trail leading through varied local scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Mountain biking around Orthevielle is defined by its mix of wooded areas, agricultural plains, and riverside paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Orthevielle are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Orthevielle's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Orthevielle?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike routes documented on komoot around Orthevielle, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, including wooded areas, agricultural fields, and riverside paths.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Orthevielle?
A: Yes, Orthevielle is particularly well-suited for beginners and families, with 14 easy routes available. These trails often feature gentle terrain through kiwi orchards and along river valleys. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974630823/kiwi-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kiwi Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 17.0-mile (27.4 km) path that winds through the distinctive agricultural plains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Orthevielle?
A: The terrain around Orthevielle is diverse, characterized by a mix of wooded parcels, open agricultural fields (including kiwi orchards), and paths along the Gaves Réunis river. While generally flatter, the region offers gentle rises and varied surfaces, providing a pleasant cycling experience through a 'green environment'.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Orthevielle?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Orthevielle are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24657510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cannon at Pont de Laxague – View of Château de Gramont loop from Sames&lt;/a&gt; is a 14.0-mile (22.5 km) circular trail that showcases varied local scenery.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails around Orthevielle?
A: The trails offer scenic views of the local landscape, including kiwi orchards and the Gaves Réunis river. You can also encounter historical landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974817509/vers-le-chateau-de-gramont-a-came" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vers le château de Gramont à Came&lt;/a&gt; route offers views towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5091791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gramont Castle in Bidache&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4838365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saubusse&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide of Hastingues&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for more advanced mountain bikers near Orthevielle?
A: While Orthevielle itself offers more gentle rides, its strategic location provides a gateway to more challenging mountainous areas. The Pyrenees, known for more intense enduro and downhill trails with significant elevation changes, are approximately two hours away, offering options for advanced riders seeking greater challenges.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Orthevielle?
A: The 'green environment' of Orthevielle makes it suitable for mountain biking through much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially on shaded routes or during cooler parts of the day, as the region is known for its agricultural plains and wooded areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Orthevielle?
A: The mountain bike routes in Orthevielle are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse terrain, which includes a mix of wooded areas, agricultural plains, and riverside paths, providing varied and scenic rides for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or offer waterside views?
A: Yes, the southern border of Orthevielle is defined by the Gaves Réunis river, and several routes incorporate riverside paths. These trails offer pleasant waterside views and a different riding experience. The wider region of Landes also features accessible waterways, enhancing the natural beauty of the routes.

Q: Are there any specific trails that highlight the 'Valley of the Kiwi'?
A: Absolutely. Orthevielle is located in the 'Valley of the Kiwi,' and some routes specifically wind through these distinctive orchards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974630823/kiwi-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kiwi Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering an immersive experience through the region's fertile plains and kiwi plantations.

Q: What is the typical duration and distance of mountain bike trails in Orthevielle?
A: The mountain bike trails in Orthevielle vary in length and duration. Many easy routes range from 14 to 18 miles (22 to 30 km) and can be completed in approximately 1 hour 15 minutes to 2 hours, depending on your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974817509/vers-le-chateau-de-gramont-a-came" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vers le château de Gramont à Came&lt;/a&gt; trail is 18.9 miles (30.3 km) and typically takes about 1 hour 54 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Beyond the scenic landscapes, you can find several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5091791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gramont Castle in Bidache&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark visible from some routes. Additionally, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide of Hastingues&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7555712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Traditional Boats of the Adour&lt;/a&gt; are nearby attractions that add cultural context to your ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Loop of Peyrehorade?
faqs.answer: Most riders can expect to complete this mountain bike loop in about 1 hour and 47 minutes. This is an estimate, and your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The Loop of Peyrehorade is classified as an easy mountain bike ride. It's suitable for all skill levels, including beginners and families, and doesn't require a high level of fitness.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for the Loop of Peyrehorade?
faqs.answer: The starting point of the route is conveniently located right next to a parking lot, making access straightforward. You won't need to worry about shuttles for this loop.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the loop?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse mix of surfaces on this loop. It includes sections of road, paths, streets, state roads, and even some singletrack. Expect to ride on asphalt, paved, unpaved, and natural sections throughout the route.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Loop of Peyrehorade?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through various types of terrain, including roads and paths, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near roads, to ensure their safety and respect for others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Loop of Peyrehorade?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty and varied terrain, the loop can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so consider riding earlier in the morning or later in the afternoon.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Loop of Peyrehorade. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: As you ride, you might pass by interesting spots such as the Promenade de la Pecherie, the historic Abbaye de Sordes, and the charming Washhouse of Sorde-l'Abbaye.

faqs.question: Does the Loop of Peyrehorade connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Cités et bastides, Piste des Kiwis, Promenade des Tilhoulès, and Chemin du Mounicou, among others, for short stretches.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! With its 'easy' difficulty rating, modest elevation gain, and diverse but manageable terrain, the Loop of Peyrehorade is an excellent choice for families looking for an enjoyable mountain biking outing together.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while riding?
faqs.answer: The loop takes you through a pleasant mix of local countryside, likely offering views of fields, smaller wooded areas, and glimpses of residential or village settings around Peyrehorade. It's a good way to experience the local landscape.
